consequences: Monitor graphs paint opaque full-width rows, startup avoids WebGPU probing, routing and cycling stay metrics-only, and Three rendering remains available on surfaces designed to showcase it. The intentional code deletion exceeds the generic guard's 200-line deletion cap but has zero blocking rollback risks and focused regression/runtime evidence.
context: The live monitor intentionally composited Three ASCII scenes beneath fallback text graphs. Five default panes selected Three scenes, producing mixed layers and unnecessary WebGPU startup for a metrics dashboard.
decision: The live monitor uses only the metric visualization catalog, renders through a monitor policy that rejects stale Three selections, and no longer constructs ThreePanelView or exposes Three-only controls. Dedicated Three demos, Neon, and the API workbench retain the renderer.
title: Keep the live system monitor separate from Three ASCII scenes
